free_text: Measurements of evaporation residues to investigate
lower limiting angular momenta in fusion reactions.
counter telescope consisting of a
dE ionization chamber and a 300 um thick silicon
detector of surface barrier type (E) placed inside the
ionization chamber.
Two monitor counters were set at forward angles in
order to normalize relative yields of evaporation
residues and the elastic scattering as well as to
monitor the beam position.
Statistical model calculations, Time-dependent
Hartree-Fock calculations. Measured velocity spectra,
deduced fusion limiting angular momenta.
Absolute cross sections of the elastic scattering were
measured at incident energies of 90, 100, 120 and
130 MeV to find a proper set of optical parameters.
Total error. Experimental errors of the
measured cross sections of ER are mainly due to
Uncertainties in the estimation of the elastic
scattering cross sections at forward angles.
Private communication.
Data from the authors.

150 ug/cm2 and 140 ug/cm2, respectively.
